summary: Test_selectReport additions fully satisfy Task 03. All seven new table-driven cases call the real selectReport and assert on its actual return value (no mocks). The five valid forms (-B, -L, -J c, -J t, -X j) and both invalid cases (-J x, -X z asserting "") are present, correct, and aligned with the implementation and view.New()/describeReport keys. Tests pass.

findings.issue: The table-driven test uses a bare loop with assert.Equal and no per-case name/identifier. When a case fails, the failure message shows only got/want strings with no indication of which opts triggered it, making diagnosis harder as the table grows (now ~29 rows). This is a pre-existing pattern, not introduced by this change.
findings.recommendation: Optionally adopt subtests: add a `name` field to the struct and wrap the assertion in `t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T){ assert.Equal(t, tc.want, selectReport(tc.opts)) })`. Not required for this minimal-change CLI task; flagged for awareness only.
